title: Sorting Array of Integers with Incorrect Sort Order

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a bubble sort algorithm to sort the array in ascending order. However, there is a bug in the code that causes the array to be sorted in descending order instead. Fix the bug to correct the sorting order.
statement.inputFormat: The function will be passed a reference to an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The function should modify the input array to be sorted in ascending order.
statement.notes: The function should not use any additional data structures such as lists or sets.

examples.explanation: The input array is sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Think about the condition for the while loop

editorial.title: Editorial for Incorrect Sort Order
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to compare adjacent elements and swap them if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force solution would involve using a different sorting algorithm such as quicksort or mergesort.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is the bubble sort algorithm with the correct implementation.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: To prove the correctness of the algorithm, we can show that it satisfies the properties of a sorting algorithm.
editorial.dryRun: To run a dry test, we can use a sample input array such as [1, 5, 3, 2, 4] and verify that the output array is sorted in ascending order.
